Manchester City and Manchester United are set to go head-to-head for Harry Maguire now that England’s Nations League campaign is over, Sky Sports News understands.

The Manchester clubs want to sign the England defender this summer and are prepared to enter talks over a deal that would likely smash the record for an English defender.

Maguire’s current club Leicester do not want to sell him, however, and it is understood they have therefore not put a valuation on the 26-year-old.

But one source has told Sky Sports News the Foxes believe he is worth in the region of £80million – more than the world-record £75m fee Liverpool paid for Virgil van Dijk last year.

Both City and United are understood to be reluctant to pay that much – but any fee would almost certainly break the £50m City spent on signing Kyle Walker from Tottenham in 2017

JUVE CHIEF IN LONDON FOR POGBA TALKS

Juventus’s Sporting Director Fabio Paratici has travelled to the London offices of Manchester United for face-to-face talks over the potential transfer of Paul Pogba, according to Sky in Italy.

Talks are at an early stage, with Juve’s Joao Cancelo understood to be part of negotiations. Man City have cooled their interest in the Portuguese right back because of Juve’s £44m asking price – and the fact Danilo remains at the Etihad, having failed to agree a move to Inter Milan.

Paratici’s London stopover also included meetings with Chelsea to find a solution to the impasse of potential compensation payments for Maurizio Sarri.

Chelsea remain unwilling to let the Italian return to Serie A without sufficient compensation being paid by Juve. Chelsea are particularly keen to recoup funds on Sarri, having paid out £9m to Antonio Conte – while facing a possible £3.5m compensation bill from Derby if they pursue Frank Lampard as a replacement.

Paratici has had a busy few days. His trip across Europe has included a stop-off in Lyon for talks over the possible signing of Tanguy N’Dombele. Tottenham remain the favourites to sign the highly-rated 22-year old midfielder.

Manchester City have agreed to pay Atletico Madrid midfielder Rodri’s release clause and the player is in contract talks with the club, Sky Sports News understands.

The 22-year-old has been Pep Guardiola’s number one target to cover the holding midfield role at the Etihad, the priority position to fill after missing out to Chelsea for Jorginho last season.

Diego Simeone wanted to keep the former Villarreal man who made 34 appearances for the club and cost Atletico £17m last year but his release clause, around £61m, has allowed the player to force the move to the Premier League.

RICE NOT FOR SALE, MANCHESTER DUO TOLD

West Ham have told both Manchester City and Manchester United Declan Rice is not for sale this summer after both clubs made initial inquiries about the player, Sky Sports News understands.

Rice had a breakthrough season last time out, making 34 Premier League appearances and winning his first caps with the senior England squad after switching his allegiance from Republic of Ireland.

The 20-year-old's performances caught the interest of both Manchester clubs, who we've been told have both made initial inquiries to West Ham.

But now West Ham have made it clear Rice is not going anywhere during this transfer window, with Manuel Pellegrini seeing him as a key part of his plans for next season.

It's thought City were interested in pursuing Rice if their deal for another holding midfield player - Rodri - falls through. And whilst they haven't yet triggered Atletico's £62m release clause, SSN understands Rodri has told Atletico he wants to leave.

From United's perspective, whilst Rice is an attractive prospect, we've been told that there may be other transfer priorities in other positions for Ole Gunnar Solskjaer, who isn't yet sure what his budget for new players will be, with the future of a number of Manchester United stars still in the balance.

Man City forward Leroy Sane remains Bayern Munich’s top transfer target this summer despite there being no movement on the deal. However, according to Sky in Germany sources, Sane has reservations about coming to Bundesliga.

All other Bayern transfers business this summer will depend on whether they can secure Sane. Leipzig striker Timo Werner is the first back-up option for the German champions and the two clubs are in agreement, with the player having agreed terms.

However, with Robert Lewandowski, Thomas Muller and Kingsley Coman all playing in a similar position to Werner, his transfer might have to wait until next summer when he can move for free.

Yaya Toure set to join Chinese second division side
City legend Yaya Toure is set to continue his career in the Chinese second division with Qingdao Huanghai, according to the Daily Mail.

Reports suggested that Toure would retire from football last month but he set the record straight while on punditry duty at City’s FA Cup win and insisted he would carry on playing.

The Ivorian has been without a club since his contract was mutually terminated with Olympiacos in December but he has been training with the club in China and is ‘close to agreeing a deal.’

Man City goalkeeper Aro Muric to sign for Nottingham Forest
Aro Muric will join Nottingham Forest on a season’s loan.

But Manchester City still have extremely high hopes that the 20-year-old Kosovo international will return to fight it out with Ederson in their first team squad next summer.

Muric spent the season as under-study to the Brazil international, and performed well when given his chance in the first team in five Carabao Cup ties – including a heroic display in a penalty shoot-out win at Leicester.

But with Claudio Bravo expected back following a nine-month absence with an achilles tendon rupture, City did not want to relegate Muric back to number three.

Instead, they offered 6ft6in Muric on loan, and with Preston North End also keen, Forest won the race.

Bravo is expected to leave next summer, when he will be 37 and at the end of his contract, freeing up space for Muric in the squad – providing his loan goes well.

【Now Sports-體育】曼城剛以高達7000萬歐元買斷馬德里體育會中場洛迪卡斯簡迪，他的朋友靴拿利祖就大爆其私生活跟一般球員大有分別。

22歲的洛迪卡斯簡迪（Rodri）雖然已經名成利就，但仍然十分看重學業；修讀商業經濟的他從未錯過任何一課，而且仍然住在大學宿舍，「剛開始時，大家看到洛迪打乒乓球及洗衣服時都十分震驚。」

靴拿利祖指，洛迪就算成為了職業球員，亦已入選了西班牙國家隊，但這些成就都沒有令他改變，仍然腳踏實地：「洛迪經常都說，他的生活不只得足球，希望可以跟正常人一樣過日子。」

「洛迪的汽車仍然是那輛二手的Opel Corsa。他們建議洛迪買更好的，但他不明白為甚麼要花錢於汽車之上。他說有些朋友瘋狂買車，但他認為車只是用來從A點去到B點，就此而已。」靴拿利祖續說。

Galatasaray have made Fabian Delph a ‘primary’ transfer target this summer, according to reports in Turkey.

Delph has fallen down the pecking order at City after a season on the fringes, and the arrival of Angelino has made him fourth choice at left back.

Reports had suggested Delph had already emptied his locker at City in anticipation of a move away, and now Galatasaray are named as a possible destination.

Turkish outlet Aksam (via SportWitness) say Galatasaray boss Fateh Terim has identified Delph as a replacement for former City man Fernando - linked with Sevilla - and has made Delph his top target.

The report adds Delph wants to be part of England’s Euro 2020 squad next summer so would be open to a move.

Gabriel Jesus could return to Manchester City a different player to the one who struggled through parts of last season, according to Brazilian legend Rivaldo.

The 22-year-old has enjoyed a fine Copa America and he scored one and created one in the 2-0 semi-final success against Argentina.

“Gabriel Jesus has impressed me. We all know that things didn’t went well for Jesus at the 2018 World Cup, as he was a regular first choice player and didn’t score a single goal,” Rivaldo told Betfair .

“But Tite kept his confidence in the kid and Jesus has rewarded his manager. He is still a young player with great potential and time to evolve - he will have many more opportunities.

“Jesus had a tough season, losing some importance in the Manchester City team, but Guardiola will be happy seeing him shining for the Brazilian national team and gaining confidence for next season.”
